title: amauropelta bergiana
intro: Amauropelta bergiana is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae, native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Amauropelta bergiana include relieving coughs and congestion, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Amauropelta bergiana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
